task: Realized diagnostics-only observability for the MCP OAuth2/SAML callback flow: unauthenticated log-only beacon endpoint, instrumented bridge-page script, CSP connect-src broadening, sanitized backend logging, and frontend hook diagnostics logging — without changing auth behavior (EPMCDME-13237).

key_reasoning.reason: DELTA +1 vs initial (L 4 -> XL 5). The realized diff touched 11 files total (1 new + 10 modified) across two repos, landing in the XL 11-15 band; the initial estimate of ~7-9 modified + 1-2 new fell in the L band. New: backend _diagnostics.py. Modified backend: router.py, _callback_pages.py, _constants.py, _oauth2_callback.py, test_oauth2_callback_bridge.py, test_saml_acs_bridge.py. Modified frontend: useAuthCallbackListener.ts, useMCPAuthPrompt.ts plus both Vitest specs. The instrumentation spread wider than estimated because diagnostics logging reached both frontend hooks and four test files in addition to the five core backend touch points.

Confirmed L (4), no delta. Full-stack additive instrumentation across ~7 components and 2 layers: a new diagnostics endpoint (router.py), a small self-contained diagnostics module (_diagnostics.py: payload model + sanitizer + response builder), beacon emission in the bridge-script generator and page-served logging (_callback_pages.py), CSP/path constants (_constants.py), callback-orchestration entry/verified logging (_oauth2_callback.py), and two instrumented frontend hooks. Held at L rather than XL because nothing was restructured and no new abstraction was introduced — _diagnostics.py is a tiny log-only unit.

Confirmed L (4), no delta. Security-sensitive OAuth2/SAML callback surface: a new UNAUTHENTICATED /oauth2/callback-diagnostics endpoint (router.py) hardened with pydantic extra=ignore, per-field max_length caps, a Literal result, a 204 empty response, and MCP-auth gating. CSP was broadened (connect-src 'self') on a hardened page. The realized risk materialized: code review found and fixed CR-001, a log-injection finding (CWE-117), now mitigated by _sanitize_log_value neutralizing CR/LF/control chars at the source. Base M (reversible, log-only, no migration, easy rollback) bumped to L by the Security red flag — validated by the actual finding.

red_flags_applied: Technical Risk bumped from M (3) to L (4): Security/Compliance red flag — security-sensitive OAuth2/SAML callback surface, a new unauthenticated log-only endpoint requiring hardening, CSP broadening, and secret-leakage avoidance. Validated by the realized CR-001 log-injection (CWE-117) finding, found and fixed during review via _sanitize_log_value.
